title: French Onion Soup

overview.description: A rich French soup of deeply caramelised onions in beef stock, topped with toasted bread and melted cheese.

method: Melt the butter in a large heavy-based saucepan over a medium-low heat.
Add the onions with a pinch of salt.
Cook gently for 40–50 minutes, stirring regularly, until deeply golden and caramelised.
Sprinkle over the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the white wine and scrape the bottom of the pan to deglaze.
Cook until the wine has reduced by about half.
Add the beef stock, bay leaf and thyme.
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 25 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf and thyme stems.
Season to taste with salt and black pepper.
Top each bowl with a slice of toasted baguette.
Cover generously with the grated Gruyère.
Grill until the cheese is bubbling and golden.

planner.freezer-label: freeze before adding bread and cheese

learning.did-you-know: French onion soup dates back centuries, but the gratinéed version with bread and cheese became especially popular in Paris during the 19th century.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to properly caramelise onions, deglaze a pan and finish a soup under the grill.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A classic soup that showcases the flavour of homemade beef stock and demonstrates how patience transforms simple ingredients.
chapman-tips: Don't rush the onions—deep caramelisation is where almost all of the flavour comes from.
Homemade beef stock makes a noticeable difference.
Gruyère is traditional, but a good mature Cheddar also works well.
Freeze the soup before adding the bread and cheese, then finish under the grill after reheating.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the soup base in portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently and add fresh bread and cheese before serving.
